title: thymus serpyllum subsp. richardii
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. richardii, also known as wild thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, native to Europe and Asia, and is characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and leaves. The medicinal properties of Thymus serpyllum subsp. richardii include its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to alleviate respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds, as well as its use in treating digestive issues, stress, and anxiety. Thymus serpyllum subsp. richardii can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or essential oil, which can be used to promote relaxation, boost the immune system, and support overall well-being.
